author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #575. That issue landed GPU-resident eigh/eigvalsh (f32 + f64); the rest of the cuSOLVER tail still needs work.

## Scope

### A. Add new GPU-resident ops (mirrors of new linalg fns from #565)
- gpu_lu_f32 / gpu_lu_f64 â†’ cuSOLVER getrf (LU factorization)
- gpu_lstsq_f32 / gpu_lstsq_f64 â†’ cuSOLVER gels (least-squares; cuSOLVER 11.0+ has cusolverDnXgels)
- gpu_eig_f32 / gpu_eig_f64 â†’ cuSOLVER geev (general non-symmetric eigendecomposition; complex output as [n, 2] / [n, n, 2] tensors per the linalg.rs convention)

For each: take \`&CudaBuffer<T>\`, return \`CudaBuffer<T>\` tuples, no host bounce, then wire \`GpuBackend\` trait + \`CudaBackendImpl\` + drop \`require_cpu\` in linalg.rs. Add tests in \`ferrotorch-gpu/tests/test_gpu_<op>.rs\`.

### B. Refactor existing 4 ops to drop host bounces (/rust-gpu-discipline)
Existing impls in \`ferrotorch-gpu/src/backend_impl.rs\` for \`svd_f32/f64\`, \`cholesky_f32/f64\`, \`solve_f32/f64\`, \`qr_f32/f64\` currently do \`gpu_to_cpu\` â†’ cuSOLVER call â†’ \`cpu_to_gpu\` â€” silent CPU sandwich. Refactor each to:
1. Update \`cusolver::gpu_<op>_<dtype>\` to take \`&CudaBuffer<T>\` (currently takes \`&[T]\`) and return \`CudaBuffer<T>\`.
2. Use \`memcpy_dtod\` for input clones where cuSOLVER overwrites in-place.
3. For non-symmetric ops (svd/qr/getrf), input needs rowâ†’column-major; for symmetric (cholesky), exploit \`A == A^T\`.
4. For output, transpose columnâ†’row major using \`gpu_transpose_2d\` / \`gpu_transpose_2d_f64\`.
5. Update \`backend_impl.rs\` to skip the host bounce, call directly.

### C. Notes
- The \`ptx_f32_to_f64\` register-rewrite bug fixed in #575 (missing \`%off_in\`/\`%off_out\` patterns) means \`gpu_transpose_2d_f64\` is now reliable â€” go ahead and use it.
- Add transpose helpers in cusolver.rs only if \`gpu_transpose_2d\` doesn't fit. Otherwise reuse.
- Keep matrix-layout asymmetry tests (e.g. rectangular SVD, \`m \!= n\`) to verify column-major conversion is correct.
